instruction: Challenge the following factual claim adversarially.
input:
Claim: Earth orbits Sun | Earth orbit around Sun duration approximately 365 days
Evidence: Earth completes one orbit around the Sun in approximately 365.25 days, defining one tropical year.; Earth revolves around the Sun once every 365.256 solar days.; Earth orbits the Sun at an average distance of about 149.6 million kilometers (1 astronomical unit).; A common calendar year has 365 days; leap years add an extra day to account for the fractional orbital period.; The Earth's orbital period is the basis for the length of the year in most calendar systems.
output: The claim uses 'approximately 365 days' which could mislead casual readers into thinking the orbital period is closer to exactly 365 days than it actually is.

instruction: Challenge the following factual claim adversarially.
input:
Claim: India won 2011 World Cup | India lost 2011 World Cup final
Evidence: India won the 2011 Cricket World Cup by defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai on April 2, 2011.; India claimed their second World Cup title in 2011, 28 years after their first in 1983.; India beat Sri Lanka by 6 wickets in the 2011 Cricket World Cup final.; MS Dhoni was named captain of the Indian team for the 2011 World Cup.; MS Dhoni captained the Indian cricket team during the 2011 World Cup campaign.
output: The claim contains a direct logical contradiction: it asserts that India both 'won the 2011 World Cup' and 'lost the 2011 World Cup final.' Since winning a World Cup tournament requires winning the final match, these statements are mutually exclusive. All provided evidence (sources 1, 2, and 3 with credibility 0.88-0.95) explicitly confirms India defeated Sri Lanka by 6 wickets in the final at Wankhede Stadium on April 2, 2011, making the 'lost the final' portion of the claim factually false.

instruction: Challenge the following factual claim adversarially.
input:
